Class: StraightKeyer

StraightKeyer(output)

A straight keyer.

This is one or more relays wired in parallel. Each relay has an associated input key. You press any key, and it starts transmitting until all keys are released.

Constructor

new StraightKeyer(output)

Parameters:
Name Type Description
output Transmitter

Transmitter object
Source:
  • keyers.mjs, line 59

Methods

Key(key, pressed)

React to a key being pressed.

Parameters:
Name Type Description
key number

Which key was pressed
pressed bool

True if the key was pressed

d Duration

  • keyers.mjs, line 102

TrackPhysicalKey(key, pressed)

Track physical key press/release for safety timeout This is separate from Tx() because keyers like Bug pulse rapidly

Parameters:
Name Type Description
key number

Key number
pressed bool

True if key is pressed
Source:
  • keyers.mjs, line 142

Tx(n, closed)

Close a transmit relay.

In most of these keyers, you have multiple things that can transmit. In the circuit, they'd all be wired together in parallel. We instead keep track of relay state here, and start or stop transmitting based on the logical of of all relays.

Parameters:
Name Type Description
n number

Relay number
closed bool

True if relay should be closed
Source:
  • keyers.mjs, line 195

TxClosed(n) → {bool}

Returns the state of a single transmit relay.

If n is not provided, return the state of all relays wired in parallel.

Parameters:
Name Type Default Description
n number null

Relay number
Source:
  • keyers.mjs, line 128
Returns:

True if relay is closed

Type
bool
